title: Sea salt caramels
subtitle: Soft, chewy caramels with flaked sea salt
excerpt: Sea salt caramels are chewy caramel sweets made by cooking sugar, cream, and butter to firm-ball stage, then pouring the mixture into a tin to set. Flaked sea salt on the top cuts through the richness. They stay soft at room temperature and need to be cut and wrapped to stop them sticking together. A sugar thermometer is essential.
sourceNotes: Salted caramels are a French confectionery tradition widely popularised from the late twentieth century onward, particularly in Brittany where salted butter is traditional. The formula here follows the standard cream-and-sugar caramel documented in patisserie texts. The sea salt finish is a modern and now standard variation.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Wrap cut caramels in waxed paper; they keep at room temperature for 2 weeks.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: firm-ball stage, 122°C on the sugar thermometer; for a softer caramel, pull at 118°C

content.text: Sea salt caramels are cooked to firm-ball stage, 122°C, which gives a soft, yielding chew when cut and wrapped. Pulled at 118°C they will be slightly softer; at 126°C they will be firmer and harder to cut cleanly at room temperature. The sea salt goes on top of the poured caramel before it sets, so every square has a visible flake on the top face.
Sugar at caramel stage causes serious burns. If hot caramel touches skin, run cold water over it for at least ten minutes and seek medical care if needed.

Combine the sugar and glucose syrup in a medium, heavy saucepan over medium heat. Do not stir once the sugar starts to melt; swirl the pan if needed. Cook to a deep amber caramel. Meanwhile, warm the cream in a small saucepan until steaming. When the caramel is amber, carefully pour in the warm cream; it will bubble violently. Stir in the butter. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches 122°C on a sugar thermometer. The clip-to-the-side method is correct; do not let the tip touch the pan base.

Line a 20 cm square tin with baking paper lightly oiled. Pour the caramel in and leave for 5 minutes. Scatter flaked sea salt evenly over the top. Leave at room temperature for 2 hours, then refrigerate for 1 hour to set firm enough to cut. Cut into squares with a lightly oiled knife. Wrap each caramel individually in a square of waxed paper.
